sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The particle_exp_mc20.pyx module defines a collection of specialized particle classes tailored for experimental Monte Carlo 20 (MC20) events within the AnalysisG framework. These classes, including Top, Child, PhysicsDetector, PhysicsTruth, Electron, Muon, and Jet, all inherit from the generic ParticleTemplate. They encapsulate specific C++ particle types, providing a high-performance and Python-friendly interface to detailed particle information relevant to MC20 analyses.

sect1.title: Specialized Particle Classes
sect1.para: Each class below is a cdef class, indicating its implementation as a Cython extension type. They all inherit from ParticleTemplate, gaining its base functionalities for kinematic, quantum, and relational properties. Their primary role is to wrap a specific C++ particle object, allowing for specialized data access and manipulation.

listitem.para: Introduction: Represents a top quark particle in experimental MC20 events.
__cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ top object and assigns its pointer to self.p. It then upcasts self.p to a particle_template* and assigns it to self.ptr.
__init__(self): Python initializer, intentionally empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ top object via self.ptr.
Introduction: Represents a child particle in a decay chain within experimental MC20 events.
__cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ child object and assigns its pointer to self.p. It then upcasts self.p to a particle_template* and assigns it to self.ptr.
__init__(self): Python initializer, intentionally empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ child object via self.ptr.
Introduction: Represents a particle as observed by the detector (reconstructed level) in experimental MC20 events.
__cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ physics_detector object and assigns its pointer to self.p. It then upcasts self.p to a particle_template* and assigns it to self.ptr.
__init__(self): Python initializer, intentionally empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ physics_detector object via self.ptr.
Introduction: Represents a truth-level particle (from simulation) in experimental MC20 events.
__cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ physics_truth object and assigns its pointer to self.p. It then upcasts self.p to a particle_template* and assigns it to self.ptr.
__init__(self): Python initializer, intentionally empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ physics_truth object via self.ptr.
Introduction: Represents an electron particle in experimental MC20 events.
__cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ electron object and assigns its pointer to self.p. It then upcasts self.p to a particle_template* and assigns it to self.ptr.
__init__(self): Python initializer, intentionally empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ electron object via self.ptr.
Introduction: Represents a muon particle in experimental MC20 events.
__cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ muon object and assigns its pointer to self.p. It then upcasts self.p to a particle_template* and assigns it to self.ptr.
__init__(self): Python initializer, intentionally empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ muon object via self.ptr.
Introduction: Represents a jet particle in experimental MC20 events.
__cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ jet object and assigns its pointer to self.p. It then upcasts self.p to a particle_template* and assigns it to self.ptr.
__init__(self): Python initializer, intentionally empty.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ jet object via self.ptr.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
All classes in this module leverage Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class ...: Declares each class as a Cython extension type.
cdef <cpp_type> *p: A C++ pointer to a specific particle type (e.g., top*, electron*), managed by the Cython class.
self.ptr = <particle_template*>(self.p): This line demonstrates C++ upcasting, where a pointer to a derived class (e.g., top*) is assigned to a pointer to its base class (particle_template*). This allows the specialized particle classes to fulfill the ParticleTemplate interface while internally managing their specific C++ particle data.
from AnalysisG.events.exp_mc20.particle_exp_mc20 cimport *: Imports the C++ particle class declarations from their .pxd file.
from AnalysisG.core.particle_template cimport ParticleTemplate: Imports the base ParticleTemplate class, from which all these specialized classes inherit.
